title: Royalty Attribution and Payout Reconciliation

landmines.name: overlapping-subscription-fanout
landmines.kills: Joining every active subscription (no precedence tiebreak) double-counts royalties for listeners whose paid periods overlap; the highest-precedence plan must be picked once. See naive/1_between_fanout.sql.

landmines.name: not-in-with-null-artist
landmines.kills: artist_payouts has NULL-artist adjustment rows, so NOT IN for the never-paid set returns empty and drops every 'missing' discrepancy; an anti-join / NOT EXISTS is required. See naive/2_not_in_never_paid.sql.

landmines.name: null-ended-at-dropped
landmines.kills: Omitting the NULL guard on ended_at drops every play made under a still-active (open-ended) subscription, erasing most current royalty. NULL ended_at means still active. See naive/3_null_ended_dropped.sql.

landmines.name: correlated-plan-resolver-tle
landmines.kills: Resolving the active plan with a per-play correlated subquery is O(plays x subscriptions) and times out on the multi-million-row hidden fixture; the reference resolves all plans in one windowed pass. See naive/4_correlated_plan_tle.sql (correct output, TLE).

notes: Date/timestamp logic is portable via CONCAT(col,'') ISO-text coercion + lexical comparison (verified identical on sqlite, duckdb, postgres). mssql omitted: needs SUBSTRING/'+'-concat and TOP instead of this form. computed_usd/paid_usd are rounded to cents; classification uses a strict 1-cent tolerance so sub-cent float-order wobble never flips a category (real discrepancies are >=10%). naive/1 (fan-out) and naive/3 (null ended_at) require overlapping subs / open-ended subs to diverge, which are present at every real scale (proven divergent on the micro worked-example across all three engines and at hidden scale); naive/2 requires the NULL-artist payout row (present at every scale, proven divergent on the sample); naive/4 matches the reference output but is the designated TLE naive-slow for section-6 calibration.
